schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment_not_charity). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — Zuckerberg LLC-opacity test case + regen_v3@sha256-919ca20254936788 + regen_v3@sha256-80dea5cd898db9ac

person.wealth_source: Meta Platforms co-founder & CEO; ~13% ownership stake (as of Nov 2025 Bloomberg filing); also Zuckerberg private investments / family office

net_worth.range_note: Wide 2026 spread: Forbes RTB showed $251.7B late January 2026 (rank #4, passing Bezos); as of 9 April 2026 a Forbes-cited summary had him at $210.2B (#5); independent estimator at $270B. Forbes 2026 profile page ranks him #5. We use $230B as midpoint. Wikipedia cites $251B as of October 2025 (#3 globally at peak). The ~25% spread mirrors the Ellison case — single-point denominators are unstable.

sources.retrieval_note: Forbes profile page blocked WebFetch with 403; Brave search snippet confirms rank #5 on 2026 list.
Full profile blocked 403; Brave snippet confirms ~13% Meta stake per Nov 2025 filing.

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Concentrated in Meta Class A/B stock (~13% of Meta; Class B carries 10x voting power). Divesting meaningfully would dilute his voting control, which he has historically not done. Apply 20% liquidity discount for 'giving capacity now' calculations; most wealth is locked in the voting stake.

cited_events.recipient: Various (recipients not disclosed in ProPublica summary; full 990-PF schedule would itemize)
cited_events.notes: CZI Foundation 2024 filing: total expenses $323.0M; charitable disbursements $388.7M; total contributions received (transfer_in) $690.5M.

cited_events.donor_entity: Mark Zuckerberg / Priscilla Chan (personal, via Meta stock)
cited_events.dedup_note: CRITICAL: These are transfers INTO CZIF (contributions received), NOT out-the-door giving. Per dedup rule, only grants_out count toward observable giving. Double-counting risk if these rows are added to zuck_czif_YYYY grant_out events. ~$5.29B transfers in vs. ~$1.87B grants out 2017-2024 — ~$3.4B sits inside the foundation as undeployed assets, consistent with CZIF's $7.5B 2024 asset base.

cited_events.amount_usd_note: 18 million Facebook shares, valued ~$500M at December 2012 donation date
cited_events.dedup_note: This is a transfer INTO a DAF (SVCF). Under v3 methodology this is a transfer_in-equivalent for the Zuckerberg CZI DAF bucket — the DAF receives it but grants-out from SVCF Zuckerberg DAF accounts are donor-opaque. We record as direct_gift because it left Zuckerberg's control AND is tax-deductible, but the ultimate disbursement path is not publicly traceable.

cited_events.amount_usd_note: 18 million Facebook shares, valued ~$990M at Dec 2013; largest charitable gift on public record that year
cited_events.dedup_note: Same as zuck_svcf_2012 — transfer into DAF. Counted as direct_gift (tax-deductible transfer out of personal holdings) but grants-out-of-DAF are opaque.

cited_events.classification_note: Pre-CZI gift. Widely criticized ex-post for poor outcomes but documented at announcement.

cited_events.amount_usd_note: Largest individual donation to a U.S. public hospital at the time

cited_events.recipient: Center for Tech and Civic Life + Center for Election Innovation & Research (nonpartisan election administration nonprofits)
cited_events.classification_note: The so-called 'Zuckerbucks'. Both recipients are 501(c)(3) nonpartisan nonprofits — counts as charitable under Forbes/Chronicle methodology despite political framing in partisan press. Zuckerberg discontinued this category post-2020.

cited_events.recipient: Sacramento tech talent pipeline / STEM campus
cited_events.classification_note: Joint gift with Meta corporate — Zuckerberg's personal share not disclosed. If we assume 50/50 split, personal credit ~$25M. Conservative: count $25M to Zuckerberg personal.

pledges_and_announcements.status_source: Zuckerberg signed 2012 per Wikipedia; Giving Pledge website displays 2015 on pledger profile. IPS 2025 'Giving Pledge at 15' likely treats the 2015 letter date. Either way the 50% / lifetime pledge is operative.
pledges_and_announcements.pledge_letter_quote: We've had so much opportunity in our lives, and we feel a deep responsibility to make the world a better place for future generations. [...] We salute the Giving Pledge movement, and are proud to be part of its declaration that those who have been fortunate should give back at least half of their wealth during their lifetimes.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Giving Pledge official page shows year signed = 2015 (the year the public pledger letter was published). Wikipedia says Mark signed in 2012. Difference likely reflects private vs. public signing. Pledge target = 50% of lifetime wealth. At current net worth $230B, 50% target = $115B. At 2015 signing value ~$45B, 50% target = $22.5B in 2015 dollars.

pledges_and_announcements.status_source: Wikipedia: 'Pledged to transfer 99% of Facebook shares (then valued at $45 billion) to Chan Zuckerberg Initiative over their lifetimes.'
pledges_and_announcements.classification_note: CRITICAL METHODOLOGY POINT: The 2015 '99% of Meta shares' pledge is NOT a Giving Pledge commitment and NOT a commitment to a 501(c)(3). It is a commitment to CZI, whose primary vehicle is the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative, LLC — a FOR-PROFIT limited liability company. Per Wikipedia and multiple commentators: as an LLC, CZI can invest in for-profit companies, make political donations, lobby, and is NOT required to disclose its pay, grants, or any financials. IPS explicitly excludes LLC giving from generosity measures. Under Forbes/Chronicle/IPS methodology, the 99% pledge is NOT counted as charitable giving until and unless transferred to a 501(c)(3) / DAF / equivalent charitable structure. Currently ~$5.29B has flowed into CZIF (the 501(c)(3) arm) per ProPublica — that is ~2.3% of the 99% notional commitment at today's net worth.

pledges_and_announcements.claimed_amount_note: CZI self-reports 'approximately $7.22 billion in grants' committed since launch 2015, plus nearly $300M in for-profit ventures.
pledges_and_announcements.classification_note: SELF-REPORTED, UNAUDITED. CZI's $7.22B headline aggregates across CZI LLC (opaque), CZIF 501(c)(3), CZI DAF at SVCF, and CZI Advocacy 501(c)(4). ProPublica 990-PF confirms ~$1.87B in 501(c)(3) grants_out 2017-2024 — only ~26% of the self-reported total. The remaining ~74% ($5.35B) flowed through opaque LLC / DAF / c4 channels where neither recipients nor structures are independently verifiable. Under our v3 methodology, LLC-channel grants are NOT counted as charitable giving. DAF-channel grants are partial-observable (the SVCF transfer is counted as direct_gift but SVCF does not disclose which recipients got which Zuckerberg DAF dollars).

pledges_and_announcements.recipient: Chan Zuckerberg Biohub (501(c)(3) research institute, EIN 81-1669175)
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Most of this disbursement would flow through CZIF as grants_out over time and should already be captured in the annual CZIF totals. Flagging here for dedup.

$3B over 10 years science commitment. 10-year window runs 2016-2026. Partial-tracked: CZIF grants_out 2017-2024 sum ~$1.87B and CZI LLC disbursements separately self-reported. Does NOT double-count with CZIF events above.

political_giving.substitution_note: Zuckerberg is notably NOT a political-heavy donor relative to peers. The $420M 'Zuckerbucks' 2020 gift was to nonpartisan 501(c)(3) election administration nonprofits, not political spending — counted as charitable above. His 2024 'shift to libertarianism' did not materialize as large FEC donations.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Minimum floor from OpenSecrets Donor Lookup. Personal federal candidate/committee donations by Mark + Priscilla total 'at least $159,600' (OpenSecrets 2023 snapshot) — cumulative. CZI org-level contributions were $75,990 in 2024 cycle. Rounded to $250K as conservative upper on personal + CZI-attributed political 2021-2025.

foundations_active.name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: Mark Zuckerberg (Trustee) receives $0 compensation. Top compensated: Director of AI $961K, VP Science $692K, VP Science $635K (2024). No officer compensation flags at trustee level; high executive salaries reflect science-research staffing norms, not governance pay.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants_out $388.7M vs assets $7.5B = 5.2% — just above 5% legal minimum. Borderline LOW_PAYOUT flag given CZIF held $7B+ of net assets against a 99%-of-Meta-shares notional pledge.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED — CZI operates its own DAF at Silicon Valley Community Foundation; unclear from ProPublica summary whether any CZIF grants flow into the CZI-at-SVCF DAF. Plausible based on shared brand; would constitute foundation→DAF recycling flagged under DAF_TRANSFERS.

foundations_active.name: Chan Zuckerberg Biohub Inc
foundations_active.status: active (RECIPIENT of CZI grants, not a donor vehicle)
foundations_active.note: Listed for completeness. Not a donor-side vehicle — appears as a CZI grantee.

foundations_active.name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative Advocacy
foundations_active.officers_compensation: All key leadership report $0 compensation (Huezo, Castellanos, Manca, Lazarus Gardner)
foundations_active.classification_note: 501(c)(4)s can lobby and make limited political expenditures; donations to c4s are NOT tax-deductible and NOT counted under Forbes/Chronicle charitable methodology. $32.1M 2024 expense is advocacy spending, not philanthropy per se.

dafs_detected.name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative DAF at Silicon Valley Community Foundation
dafs_detected.transfers_in_note: At minimum: 2012 gift $500M + 2013 gift $990M = $1.49B of FB shares transferred to SVCF, likely into Zuckerberg-advised DAF accounts. Additional undisclosed transfers possible 2014-2025. SVCF does NOT disclose DAF-account-level activity. Grants-out from these DAF accounts are donor-opaque.
dafs_detected.classification_note: CZI explicitly lists the DAF at SVCF as one of its four funding entities. DAF contributions are tax-deductible on transfer-in but grant-out recipients are not attributable to Zuckerberg in public records.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: Zuckerberg operates at least one DAF (CZI DAF at SVCF) with at least $1.49B disclosed transfers in. DAFs are donor-opaque by design — grants-out are attributed to SVCF in public filings, not Zuckerberg. Hidden lower bound only.

llcs_philanthropic.name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative, LLC
llcs_philanthropic.legal_form: Limited Liability Company — FOR-PROFIT, NOT tax-exempt
llcs_philanthropic.filing_requirement: NONE — LLCs do not file Form 990, do not disclose compensation, do not disclose grant recipients, can invest in for-profit companies, can make political donations, can lobby. Per Wikipedia: 'is therefore not a tax-exempt organization as many philanthropies are.'
llcs_philanthropic.self_reported_note: $7.22B is CZI aggregate (LLC + Foundation + DAF + Advocacy) per chanzuckerberg.com. The LLC-only slice is not separately disclosed. ProPublica-verifiable 501(c)(3) grants 2017-2024 sum ~$1.87B, so ~$5.35B of the $7.22B aggregate flows through LLC/DAF/c4 — unverifiable channels.
llcs_philanthropic.methodology_note: IPS (July 2025 Giving Pledge at 15 report) explicitly excludes LLC giving from generosity measures. Under v3 methodology, CZI LLC disbursements are NOT counted as charitable giving until independently verifiable as grants to 501(c)(3)s. Self-reported aggregate not admissible.

detected_vehicles.llcs_note: CZI LLC is the canonical philanthropic LLC — the 2015 announcement of the vehicle is frequently cited as the point at which Silicon Valley-style 'giving LLCs' became normalized, replacing the traditional private foundation model. This is the single most important LLC_OPACITY test case in the scrooge v3 dataset.

for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.name: CZI Ventures (inside CZI LLC)
for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.methodology_note: CZI self-reports 'nearly $300M in ventures whose work aligns with our values.' These are for-profit equity investments, not charitable grants. Not counted.

detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. No public reporting of Zuckerberg in Panama/Pandora Papers. Absence of evidence only.

red_flags.evidence: Primary CZI vehicle is a for-profit LLC that does NOT file 990s, does NOT disclose compensation, does NOT disclose grant recipients. CZI self-reports $7.22B in grants since 2015, but ProPublica-verifiable 501(c)(3) grants 2017-2024 sum only ~$1.87B. The remaining ~$5.35B flows through LLC/DAF/c4 channels with no independent verification.

2015 '99% of Meta shares' pledge explicitly routes to CZI LLC (for-profit), NOT to a 501(c)(3). CZI LLC can and does hold for-profit equity investments (~$300M). Per IPS methodology, this structure is excluded from generosity measures.

Giving Pledge 50% commitment (signed 2012/2015). At current $230B net worth, 50% target = $115B. Observable verified charitable giving (CZIF grants_out + personal direct gifts + SVCF DAF transfers) ~$4.37B — roughly 3.8% of the 50% target. Even crediting the self-reported $7.22B CZI aggregate as if it were fully charitable would hit 6.3%.
red_flags.note: NOT a definitive UNFULFILLED — Zuckerberg is young (b.1984) and the pledge is lifetime. But at current age 41 and 18 years as billionaire, on-track pace would require much higher annual flow.

red_flags.evidence: $1.49B+ in disclosed personal transfers to Silicon Valley Community Foundation DAF (2012 $500M + 2013 $990M). SVCF does not disclose Zuckerberg-DAF grants-out. Additional undisclosed transfers possible. CZI explicitly includes DAF-at-SVCF as one of four funding entities.

CZIF 2024 grants_out $388.7M vs assets $7.5B = 5.2% — just barely above the 5% legal minimum for private foundations. This is a warehousing flag: CZIF has $7B+ net assets (larger than most operating foundations) but is only barely clearing minimum distribution.

Feb-May 2025: CZI eliminated its DEIA team, discontinued 'social advocacy funding' (including FWD.us immigration partnership formalized-end April 2025), slashed Bay Area nonprofit funding. Landing page scrubbed of inclusivity/economic-fairness language. Organizational pivot to 'science-first.' Whether this is a net increase in science vs. a net decrease in total is not yet quantifiable from public disclosure.

red_flags_not_present: HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (Zuckerberg/Chan take $0 at CZIF and CZI Advocacy; top staff pay is science-labor-market normal)
POLITICAL_HEAVY (direct personal FEC giving is trivial ~$160K cumulative through 2023; a sharp contrast with Ellison)
NO_OBSERVABLE_GIVING (CZIF alone has deployed $1.87B 2017-2024, well above the $100M floor)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(CZIF 990-PF grants_out 2017-2024 = $1,871M) + personal direct_gifts (Newark $100M + Ebola $25M + SF General $75M + CTCL $420M + Sacramento personal share $25M = $645M) + DAF transfers to SVCF ($1,490M) + CZI DAF-at-SVCF and c4 Advocacy and other pre-2017 CZIF grants reasonable attributed ~$359M reconciling to self-report. Rounded $4.365B. Does NOT include $7.22B CZI self-reported aggregate. Does NOT double-count transfers_in to CZIF against grants_out from CZIF.
rollup.observable_giving_high_note: If one credits CZI's self-reported $7.22B aggregate at face value (LLC + Foundation + DAF + c4 + ventures excluded), the ceiling is $7.22B. We do not credit this because IPS methodology excludes LLC giving and ~$5.35B of it is in opaque channels.
observable_giving_todo: Pull SVCF 990 to see whether Zuckerberg-advised DAF accounts disclose any grant-level detail.
Pull SEC EDGAR Form 4 G-transactions for Zuckerberg's CIK (0001548760) — verify stock gifts to SVCF, CZIF, and any non-CZI recipients.
Pull CZI Advocacy c4 990 grant detail to separate issue-advocacy spending from 501(c)(3) pass-through grants.
Dedup $420M CTCL 2020 gift against CZIF 2020 grants_out $214.7M — verify whether CTCL was a CZIF grant or came from personal funds / SVCF DAF.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.20 × $230B = $46B; tenure cap saturated (18 years). Expected = $4.6B × 0.9 adjustment for range-midpoint conservatism = $4.14B. Strict denominator.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: SVCF CZI DAF accounts have unknown grants-out. $1.49B+ disclosed transfers in; plausibly up to $500M of cumulative grants-out not yet captured in observable. Conservative upper.
hidden_upper_usd.llc_uplift_note: CZI self-reported $7.22B grants minus ProPublica-verified CZIF $1.87B minus plausible DAF attribution $1.49B minus Advocacy ~$100M-ish = ~$3.76B residual plausibly-LLC-channel. We set upper uplift at $2.855B = 75% of residual, acknowledging some is real 501(c)(3) passthrough but most is unverifiable. This is the core LLC_OPACITY dollar.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of HNW donors give anonymously sometimes. Zuckerberg is publicity-forward about most giving, so anonymous cohort is small. $100M conservative upper.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Zuckerberg raised Jewish but not disclosed as tithing. Zero.

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2 — Zuckerberg clears this comfortably (1.89x) IF self-reported $7.22B is accepted. Tier A/B/C classification here depends on how much weight is given to self-reported-unverifiable vs. independently-verified dollars. Independently verified: ~$4.37B (ratio 1.05x expected, passes 10% of liquid benchmark). But the 99% Meta pledge targets ~$227B (99% of net worth) — and observed+hidden together is $7.8B — only 3.4% of the 99% pledge target. Classification: Tier C (OPAQUE) — not verifiable-low, not verifiable-high, depends critically on how the LLC-channel dollars are resolved.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Zuckerberg is the canonical LLC_OPACITY case. His 990-PF grants alone (~$1.87B 2017-2024) would place him mid-tier. The 99% Meta shares pledge, if fully disbursed to 501(c)(3)s over his lifetime, would make him one of the largest philanthropists in history. As of 2026-04, only ~2.3% of that notional has reached the 501(c)(3) arm of CZI. The rest sits in a for-profit LLC whose grantees we cannot independently verify. Readers should understand this as: 'Signed the Giving Pledge, structured primary giving vehicle as a for-profit LLC, self-reports large aggregate giving — external verification confirms less than 30% of the self-report.'

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula for continuity with current pipeline. Giving-gap = 0 because observable already exceeds 10%-of-liquid expected. Pledge-breach proration: 50% Giving Pledge not met in absolute terms even if on-track; 99% Meta pledge barely begun. Red-flag penalty maxed due to LLC_OPACITY + DAF_TRANSFERS + BORDERLINE_LOW_PAYOUT + FOR_PROFIT_PIVOT + SOCIAL_ADVOCACY_WINDDOWN. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md.
